content.text:
You are a helpful coding assistant. Be concise.

Workspace map:
- The project repo is mounted at the burrow workspace root.
- /workspace/.canopy/agent.json is the rendered agent definition (warren seeded it).
- /workspace/.mulch/expertise/<domain>.jsonl holds the project's expertise records.
- /workspace/.seeds/issues.jsonl holds the project's issue queue.

Operating contract:
- Edit files in place. Run tests when relevant.
- Quality gates are terminal, not advisory. You are NOT done until the gate exits zero. Resolve the command in this order: `$WARREN_QUALITY_GATE` if set, otherwise the command documented in CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md, otherwise fall back to `bun run check:all` or `npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm test`. Run it before committing and again before reporting completion. Do not declare the task complete, hand off, or end the session with a red gate — fix failures (including lint warnings, which CI treats as errors) until it is green. If the gate is genuinely unfixable in this run, say so explicitly and leave the work open rather than claiming success.
- Use git as you normally would. Commit your changes; warren reaps the branch and pushes upstream.
- Committing is mandatory, not the same as staging. `git add` ALONE IS NOT ENOUGH — you must run `git commit` so the work lands as a real commit. A run that ends with staged-but-uncommitted changes is treated as a FAILURE (`dropped_commit`), not a success. Before you report completion, run `git status`/`git log` and confirm your changes are in a commit, not just staged. The only exception is when you have genuinely made no file changes at all.
- Do not run `git push` yourself — warren handles the push host-side after the run terminates.

---

work on sd warren-c770. use ml. commit when done.

content.text:
warren-c770  open
Title:    Lifetime: exempt mode:"conversation" from watchdog force-fail, crash-recovery finalize, and reap workspace-destroy
Type:     task   Priority: High
Labels:   leveret

Spec: LEVERET.md §0.4 / §0.0.E / build-phase 2.

The pi-chat runtime (burrow side) stops the per-turn agent_end terminal envelope, so the run stays running across turns. Warren-side guards must stop force-killing an idle conversation run. NOTE (§0.0.E scout correction): the pause detector ALREADY exempts conversation for free (src/runs/pause.ts guards on mode !== 'batch'), and workspace GC (src/runs/reap/gc.ts) is state-keyed (safe while non-terminal). So ONLY these need an explicit mode:'conversation' skip:
- Heartbeat watchdog (src/runs/watchdog.ts): opt-in via WARREN_RUN_HEARTBEAT_TIMEOUT_MS, has NO mode exemption today — add one so an armed watchdog can't force-fail an idle conversation run.
- Reap workspace-destroy (src/runs/reap/destroy.ts): extend the existing mode:'interactive' exemption to mode:'conversation'.
- Crash recovery: bootBridges (src/server/bridges.ts) RESUMES bridges and only finalizes (burrow_run_lost) when a burrow pre-probe 404s — exempt or re-anchor mode:'conversation' rows (trigger is burrow-lost, not a blanket restart sweep).

Acceptance: an idle conversation run with the watchdog armed is not force-failed; reap does not destroy its workspace; crash-recovery does not blanket-finalize it; tests cover each guard; bun run check:all passes.
Blocked by: warren-0b91
Blocks:     warren-6ccf, warren-9d99
Created:  2026-06-06T08:25:24.345Z
Updated:  2026-06-06T08:26:06.291Z


Plan: pl-d2d9  [approved]
Plan steps (18):
  warren-0b91  [open]  DB: add conversations + messages tables and mode:"conversation" run mode, with repos (ConversationsRepo/MessagesRepo) and drift parity
  warren-9022  [open]  DB: add plots projection table (JSON-blob state + promoted scalars id/project_id/status/title/updated_at) and PlotsRepo with drift parity
  warren-7b60  [closed]  Wire a plots-projection upsert hook into the src/plot-client/ read/write paths
  warren-c770  [open]  Lifetime: exempt mode:"conversation" from watchdog force-fail, crash-recovery finalize, and reap workspace-destroy
  warren-005d  [open]  Lifetime: add conversation.idleTimeoutMs config knob + idle-timeout coordinator boot-worker that finalizes the anchoring run only
  warren-6ccf  [open]  Re-wake: spawn a fresh mode:"conversation" run that replays the DB transcript into a new pi session
  warren-e38b  [open]  Seeding: add pi_extensions agent section -> .pi/extensions/ drop in src/runs/seed.ts (mirror pi_skills/pi_prompts)
  warren-fdd9  [open]  Agent: add src/registry/builtins/leveret.ts (runtime pi-chat, network open) shipping the propose_intent extension, and append to BUILTIN_AGENTS
  warren-ce65  [open]  Conversation stream bridge: persist turns to messages, parse propose_intent from tool_execution_end details, write intent_edited(actor=leveret) host-side
  warren-af15  [open]  API: conversation endpoints (create/list/get/post-message) and hide mode:"conversation" from the Runs API
  warren-763f  [open]  UI: top-level Leveret nav entry + conversations list page
  warren-01c8  [open]  UI: Leveret split-view page — streamed chat (left), live plot intent rendered dynamically from the plot JSON shape (right), top-bar send-off buttons
  warren-756d  [open]  Send-off: open a plotSync PR carrying only the plot-state update, close the conversation, and persist the PR ref + plot_id + planner agent
  warren-b872  [open]  Send-off: PR-merge poller boot-worker (reuse src/plan-runs/pr-merge.ts) that auto-dispatches the planner run keyed on plot_id when the PR merges
  warren-6e45  [open]  UI: operator-gated "Dispatch plan" popup mirroring /plan-runs/new, wired to the existing plan-run dispatch path
  warren-d622  [open]  Retire mode=interactive/brainstorm (spawnInteractiveTurn, POST /brainstorm, reap/interactive, brainstorm builtin), keeping the no-Plot batch path byte-identical
  warren-9f47  [open]  Acceptance: add a deterministic, idempotent scenario exercising conversation -> live intent -> send-off -> dispatch under scripts/acceptance/scenarios/
  warren-8355  [open]  Release: run /release per .claude/commands/release.md with a MINOR version bump (package.json + src/index.ts) and CHANGELOG update

# Project Expertise Manifest (via Mulch)

> Manifest mode lists available domains. Load records on demand with `ml prime <domain>` or `ml prime --files <path>`.

## Quick Reference

- `ml prime <domain>` — load full records for one domain
- `ml prime --files <path>` — load records relevant to specific files
- `ml search "<query>"` — search records across domains
- `ml record <domain> --type <type> --description "..."` — store an insight
- `ml learn` — discover what to record from changed files
- `ml sync` — validate, stage, and commit .mulch/ changes

## Available Domains

- **build**: 104 records (5 patterns, 70 conventions, 17 failures, 7 decisions, 5 references) — updated just now — approaching limit
- **db**: 72 records (12 patterns, 44 conventions, 5 failures, 11 decisions) — updated just now
- **burrow-client**: 18 records (6 patterns, 10 conventions, 1 decision, 1 reference) — updated just now
- **registry**: 36 records (2 patterns, 23 conventions, 1 failure, 8 decisions, 2 references) — updated just now

- **runs**: 174 records (45 patterns, 101 conventions, 5 failures, 20 decisions, 3 references) — updated just now ⚠ consider splitting domain

- **release**: 4 records (1 pattern, 1 convention, 2 failures) — updated just now

# 🚨 SESSION CLOSE 🚨

**If you discovered insights worth preserving** — a new convention, a pattern that worked, a decision made, a failure encountered — record them before closing this session:

```bash
ml learn                              # see what files changed
ml record <domain> --type <type> ...  # record the insight

content.thinking:
I'm noticing that warren-0b91, which would add conversation mode to RUN_MODES, is still blocked and incomplete. Since c770 depends on it but I'm supposed to work on c770, I'm facing a decision: should I add the conversation mode myself, or wait? The issue mentions exempting that mode, but without it existing, the guard code that references it won't work properly.

Looking at the type system, run.mode is currently typed as "batch" | "interactive", so comparing against "conversation" would cause a TypeScript error. I could add the mode literal to RUN_MODES in columns.ts as a minimal change—since the schema files reference RUN_MODES, adding it there should propagate automatically. But that's technically part of 0b91's scope, which includes database tables and full parity. Let me check how the existing mode exemptions are structured in destroy.ts and pause.ts to understand the pattern I should follow.

arguments.command: cd /workspace && grep -rn "interactive\|mode" src/runs/reap/destroy.ts | head -30; echo "===PAUSE==="; grep -n "interactive\|batch\|mode" src/runs/pause.ts | head

content.thinking:
The blocker is that warren-0b91 hasn't been merged yet, so "conversation" doesn't exist as a RUN_MODES option. The prior steps are done, but without that change I can't reference the conversation mode. I'll add "conversation" to RUN_MODES as the minimal fix needed to move forward on c770.

Now I need to find where to add the conversation skip logic — checking the destroy.ts exemption block and the bridge reconciliation logic in bridges.ts and bridge-reconnect.ts to see where the mode check should go.

I'm working through the crash recovery logic for bridges—when a conversation run hits a 404 during pre-probe, the system needs to exempt or re-anchor rows in conversation mode rather than doing a blanket restart, with the trigger being burrow-lost specifically. The bootBridges function resumes bridges and only finalizes them when a burrow pre-probe 404s.

For a conversation run that 404s on pre-probe, I shouldn't finalize it to burrow_run_lost. Instead, I'll exempt it by skipping the bridge start but leaving the row non-terminal so re-wake can spawn a fresh one. This means skipping the finalize call with a different reason like "conversation_burrow_lost" rather than calling reconcileLostBurrowRun. The watchdog won't kill an exempt row and reap won't run, letting the re-wake step handle spawning fresh.

I need to add "conversation" to RUN_MODES as a prerequisite to make this self-contained and testable, even though there's a potential merge conflict with the other plan step that might also add it.

arguments.command: cd /workspace && grep -rn "mode === \"interactive\"\|mode === \"batch\"\|mode !== \"batch\"\|RunMode" src/ --include=*.ts | grep -v test | grep -v "\.md"

content.thinking: Adding "conversation" to RUN_MODES should be safe since the type narrowing from parseRunMode will handle the exhaustive switch checks properly. Now I'm looking at the columns.ts file to understand the context and make the minimal necessary change.

I'm weighing whether to add "conversation" to RUN_MODES now or wait for the planned step that handles database tables and the mode together. The constraint to deliver working code with passing tests pushes me toward adding it, since c770 fundamentally needs the mode to exist, but I'm second-guessing whether I'm getting ahead of the plan.

The pragmatic choice is to add the minimal mode entry here and note it in the commit—that way c770 can be completed with green gates, and I'll implement the necessary guards.

edits.newText:
* and exits. Mode is fixed at run-create time. TS-only narrowing (mx-2ab984);
 * defaults to `batch` so legacy rows written before this column existed match
 * the historical shape.
 *
 * `conversation` (LEVERET.md §0.4, warren-c770) is the pi-chat runtime mode:
 * the burrow-side agent suppresses the per-turn `agent_end` terminal envelope,
 * so the anchoring run stays non-terminal across turns. Warren-side lifetime
 * guards (watchdog, reap workspace-destroy, crash-recovery finalize) must
 * therefore exempt it — an idle conversation run is healthy, not hung.
 */
export const RUN_MODES = ["batch", "interactive", "conversation"] as const;

  * and exits. Mode is fixed at run-create time. TS-only narrowing (mx-2ab984);
  * defaults to `batch` so legacy rows written before this column existed match
  * the historical shape.
+ *
+ * `conversation` (LEVERET.md §0.4, warren-c770) is the pi-chat runtime mode:
+ * the burrow-side agent suppresses the per-turn `agent_end` terminal envelope,
+ * so the anchoring run stays non-terminal across turns. Warren-side lifetime
+ * guards (watchdog, reap workspace-destroy, crash-recovery finalize) must
+ * therefore exempt it — an idle conversation run is healthy, not hung.
  */
-export const RUN_MODES = ["batch", "interactive"] as const;
+export const RUN_MODES = ["batch", "interactive", "conversation"] as const;
 export type RunMode = (typeof RUN_MODES)[number];
 
 /**
  * Chain-kind discriminator for a run that carries a `parent_run_id`

Three warren-side lifetime guards gained an explicit conversation skip: (1) heartbeat watchdog (src/runs/watchdog.ts) — 'if (run.mode===\"conversation\") continue' before computeIdleMs so an armed WARREN_RUN_HEARTBEAT_TIMEOUT_MS can't force-fail idle conversations; (2) reap workspace-destroy (src/runs/reap/destroy.ts) — extends the interactive exemption to conversation, emitting reap.workspace_destroy_skipped reason 'conversation_run'; (3) bootBridges crash-recovery (src/server/bridges.ts) — on a pre-probe 404 for a conversation run, skip the bridge with reason 'conversation_burrow_lost' WITHOUT calling reconcileLostBurrowRun, leaving the row non-terminal so re-wake (warren-6ccf) replays the transcript. arguments.command:
cd /workspace && git add -A && git status --short && git commit -q -m "Exempt mode:\"conversation\" runs from lifetime force-fail/destroy/finalize (warren-c770)

The pi-chat runtime suppresses the per-turn agent_end terminal envelope,
so a conversation run stays non-terminal across turns. Add explicit
mode:\"conversation\" skips to the three warren-side lifetime guards that
lacked one:

- Heartbeat watchdog (src/runs/watchdog.ts): skip conversation runs before
  the idle-budget check so an armed WARREN_RUN_HEARTBEAT_TIMEOUT_MS can't
  force-fail an idle conversation.
- Reap workspace-destroy (src/runs/reap/destroy.ts): extend the existing
  interactive exemption to conversation (skip reason conversation_run).
- Crash recovery (src/server/bridges.ts): on a pre-probe 404 for a
  conversation run, skip the bridge with reason conversation_burrow_lost
  WITHOUT finalizing to burrow_run_lost; re-wake (warren-6ccf) replays the
  transcript into a fresh session.

Adds \"conversation\" to RUN_MODES (src/db/schema/columns.ts). Tests cover
each guard. bun run check:all passes." && git log --oneline -1
